questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An ideal monoatomic gas undergoes a process in which $P=3 V^3$. Choose the correct statements.
options: If volume increases then temperature will also increase
If temperature of the gas decreases, then pressure must also decrease.
For expansion, heat will have to be supplied to the gas.
If temperature increases then work done by gas is positive.
questions.solution:
$P=V^3$, for ideal gas
$\mathrm{PV}=\mathrm{nRT}$
(A) relation between V and T
$V \times\left(3 V^3\right)=n R T$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{V}^4=\left(\frac{\mathrm{nR}}{3}\right) \mathrm{T} \Rightarrow \mathrm{V}^4 \propto \mathrm{T}$
(B) Relation between P and T
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{PV}=\mathrm{nRT} \Rightarrow \mathrm{P}\left(\frac{\mathrm{P}}{3}\right)^{1 / 3}=\mathrm{nRT} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{P}^{4 / 3} \propto \mathrm{T}\end{aligned}$
(C) For expansion
$V \rightarrow$ increases
work-done : positive
internal energy : increases
Hence, heat will have to supplied to the gas.
(D) As $\mathrm{T} \propto \mathrm{V}^4$
with increase in temperature, volume increases
Hence work done is positive.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
$\mathbf{S}_{\mathbf{1}}: \quad$ The process is represents isobaric process.
$\mathbf{S}_2$ : The process represents isochoric process.
$\mathbf{S}_3$ : The process represents isothermal process.
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{S}_1$ : Isobaric process must pass through origin.
$\mathrm{S}_2$ : Isochoric process must pass through origin.
$\mathrm{S}_3$ : Straight line on PV does not represent isothermal process.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An ideal gas has molar heat capacity at constant pressure $C_p=5 R / 2$. The gas is kept in a cylindrical vessel fitted with a piston which is free to move. Mass of the frictionless piston is 9 kg . Initial volume of the gas is $0.0027 \mathrm{~m}^3$ and cross-section area of the piston is $0.09 \mathrm{~m}^2$. The initial temperature of the gas is 300 K . Atmospheric pressure $P_0=1.05 \times 10^5 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2$. An amount of $2.5 \times 10^4 \mathrm{~J}$ of heat energy is supplied to the gas such that piston move without acceleration, then
options: Initial pressure of the gas is $1.06 \times 10^5 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2 \quad$
Final temperature of the gas is 1000 K
Final pressure of the gas is $1.06 \times 10^5 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2$
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{PV}=\mathrm{nRT}$   ...(1)
Mass of piston is 9 kg \& area of piston is $0.09 \mathrm{~m}^2$
So $P=P_0+\frac{9 g}{0.09}$
$P=1.06 \times 10^5 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2$
As mass of piston is not changed \& Piston is frictionless So process is isobaric $P_f=P_f=1.06 \times 10^5 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}^2$
$\mathrm{dQ}=\mathrm{nCpdT}$    ....(2)
or $\mathrm{P}_2 \mathrm{~V}_2-\mathrm{P}_1 \mathrm{~V}_1=\mathrm{nRdT}$   ....(3)
$(3) \div(2) \quad \frac{\mathrm{P}\left(\mathrm{V}_2-\mathrm{V}_1\right)}{\mathrm{dQ}}=\frac{2}{5}$
$\begin{aligned} \Rightarrow & P\left(V_2-V_1\right)=\frac{2}{5} \times 2.5 \times 10^4 \\ & W=P \Delta V=10^4 \mathrm{~J} \quad \& \quad \text { by equation (1) }\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \quad n=\frac{1.06 \times 10^5 \times 0.0027}{R \times 300} \\ & \quad n=\frac{1.06 \times 270}{2500}=0.11448 \\ & \text { or by } d Q=n \mathrm{CpdT} \\ & \because \quad \mathrm{T}_2-\mathrm{T}_1=\frac{2.5 \times 10^4}{0.11448 \times \frac{5 R}{2}} \\ & \text { on solving } \mathrm{T}_2=10782.18 \mathrm{~K}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: During an experiment an ideal gas is found to obey a condition $\frac{P^2}{\rho}=$ constant ( $\rho=$ density of gas). The gas is initially at temperature $T_0$, pressure $P_0$ and density $\rho_0$. The gas expands such that density changes to $\frac{\rho_0}{2}$. Then which of the following is/are correct:
options: The pressure of the gas changes to $\sqrt{2} \mathrm{P}_0$
The temperature of the gas changes to $\sqrt{2} \mathrm{~T}_0$
The graph of the above process on the $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{T}$ diagram is parabola.
The graph of the above process on the $\mathrm{P}-\mathrm{T}$ diagram is hyperbola.
questions.solution:
$\frac{\mathrm{P}^2}{\rho}=$ constant
$\Rightarrow \frac{P^2(n R T)}{M P}=$ constant
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{PT}=$ constant
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
